name: Clerk MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Clerk MCP server (part of Clerk's agent toolkit) enabling AI agents to manage Clerk user data — creating, reading, updating users, managing sessions, checking permissions, and handling organization memberships.
use_cases: Managing Clerk user accounts and profiles from agent workflows
Checking user permissions and roles for authorization agents
Managing organization memberships and invitations via agents
Creating user accounts programmatically from onboarding agents
Auditing user session and authentication activity
not_for: End-user login flows (this is the Clerk Backend API)
best_when: An agent needs to manage users, check permissions, or handle authentication data in a Next.js/React app using Clerk for auth.
avoid_when: You're using Auth0, Supabase Auth, or another identity provider — use their respective MCPs.

auth.notes: Clerk Secret Key (sk_live_*) for backend API access. No fine-grained scopes — key grants full backend API access for the application.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier. MCP agent toolkit is open source. Clerk subscription required for full API access.

performance.rate_limits: Backend API: 100 req/s; well-documented rate limit headers

known_agent_gotchas: Secret Key must never be exposed client-side — backend-only usage
Development vs Production instances use different Secret Keys
User IDs are Clerk-specific (user_xxx format) — not portable across auth providers
Organization features require separate configuration — not enabled by default
Webhook events preferred for reactive workflows — polling user state is expensive
API key lacks scope granularity — full application access with single key
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Clerk is purpose-built as a security product — their own security posture is excellent. SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GDPR. Secret Key lacks scopes but overall security model is strong.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single Secret Key without scopes is a gap — Clerk is aware and working on it. SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GDPR. Auth-provider grade security.
